news.summary: The Chainalysis CEO Jonathan Levin highlighted at the 2025 Consensus crypto conference that law enforcement's success in tracing stolen funds has led to many arrests related to crypto-related kidnappings. He emphasized that some criminal organizations are unaware that cryptocurrencies are traceable, leading to their involvement in such crimes. The recent incidents include a 7 million euro ($7.8 million) kidnapping plot in Paris and a home invasion targeting crypto assets. Levin urged the crypto industry to address rising security concerns as there have been 22 recorded incidents of in-person crypto-related theft this year, according to a GitHub list. Despite law enforcement successes, Levin suggested that the industry needs to take additional measures to tackle these issues effectively. Furthermore, it was highlighted that ransom payments might be recoverable, and Levin hopes this will deter criminals from engaging in such activities. The article also mentions incidents involving high-profile individuals like Amouranth and emphasizes the importance of securing personal information to prevent such attacks.
news.headline: Chainalysis CEO Jonathan Levin sheds light on recent Paris crypto attacks. Law enforcement tracing success led to many arrests. In May, 2 attacks in Paris marked; one attempted €7.8M kidnap. 22 crypto thefts so far in 2025, down from 28 in 2024. Organized crime should note crypto traceability.

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that a record-breaking number of 300,000 new COVID-19 cases have been confirmed globally. This surge in cases has brought the total number of confirmed cases worldwide to over 10 million. The United States remains the most affected country with over 2.5 million cases and 125,000 deaths. Furthermore, several countries are considering reinstating lockdown measures to combat the spread of the virus. Meanwhile, the global death toll has exceeded 500,000, with experts emphasizing the importance of following safety guidelines to prevent further escalation of the pandemic.
news.headline: Asian markets drop as trade-truce boost fades; stocks fall.

news.summary: BitMEX introduced a new trading option, the LAUNCHCOINUSDT perpetual swap, enabling traders to leverage as high as 12.5x. This listing became available on May 15, 2025, expanding trading possibilities for users.
news.headline: BitMEX debuts LAUNCHCOINUSDT perpetual swap, enabling 12.5x leverage. Live since May 15, 2025, opening up trading options.

news.summary: In Singapore, Non-Executive Director Mr. Andrew Wolff has notified BW LPG Limited of his resignation from the Board of Directors, effective from the Annual General Meeting on May 15, 2025. The company, listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange as "BWLPG.OL" and on the New York Stock Exchange as "BWLP," expressed appreciation for Mr. Wolff's service and input during his five-year term on the board. Chair Andreas Sohmen conveyed gratitude for Mr. Wolff's commitment and contributions.
news.headline: BW LPG Limited's Non-Exec Director Andrew Wolff resigns, stepping down from the Board of Directors on 15 May 2025 after 5 years.

news.summary: European leaders have emphasized the significance of maintaining strict food safety standards, labeling them a non-negotiable priority. This stance comes amidst criticism from Trump administration officials who oppose regulations that prevent American beef and other meats from being sold in European grocery stores. The disagreement highlights a clash of views on food safety, with European standards setting a clear “red line.”
news.headline: Europeans press food safety as a trade barrier with US. Trump admin criticizes rules keeping US beef out.Characters: 100

news.summary: J&T Finance Group SE, headquartered in the Czech Republic, recently announced a deal with KBC Bank NV for the acquisition of a 98.45% stake in 365.bank a.s. The agreement involves the purchase of the stake for EUR 761 million in cash. The transaction is currently awaiting approval from regulatory and anti-trust authorities and is anticipated to be finalized by the end of the year.
news.headline: J&T Finance Group sold 98.45% stake in 365.bank to KBC for EUR 761 million, expanding KBC's presence in Slovakia.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, KBC Bank reported a net profit of 546 million euros. The total income saw growth due to increased insurance revenues, trading and fair value income, and net other income. However, net interest income and net fee and commission income slightly decreased due to seasonal factors and positive year-end effects in the previous quarter. The loan portfolio expanded by 2% quarter-on-quarter and 7% year-on-year. Customer deposits, excluding certain types, remained stable quarter-on-quarter and increased by 7% year-on-year. Operating expenses rose due to recording the bulk of bank and insurance taxes for the year. Excluding these taxes, operating expenses decreased by 8% quarter-on-quarter. Insurance service expenses and loan loss impairment charges also fell. The credit cost ratio for the quarter was just 8 basis points. KBC Bank maintained a strong solvency position with an unfloored fully loaded common equity ratio of 14.5% under Basel IV at the end of March 2025. The liquidity position was solid with an LCR of 157% and NSFR of 140%.
news.headline: KBC Group reports Q1 net profit of 546 million euros. Loan portfolio grows by 2% and customer deposits up 7% year-on-year. Operating expenses up due to taxes, but expenses fell by 8% excluding taxes. Credit cost ratio favorable at 8 basis points. Common equity ratio strong at 14.5% under Basel IV. Liquidity position solid with LCR of 157% and NSFR of 140%.

news.summary: In Shenzhen, China, on May 15, 2025, itel, a popular smart life brand, revealed the introduction of the CITY 100, the first smartphone in its new CITY Series. The itel CITY 100 sets new standards for affordable smartphones by incorporating robust features.
news.headline: itel launches CITY 100 in CITY Series, redefining budget smartphones for Gen Z with style & durability.

news.summary: On 15th May 2025, DNO ASA, a Norwegian oil and gas operator, disclosed that following the Annual General Meeting on 6th June 2024, the Board of Directors has sanctioned a dividend payout of NOK 0.3125 per share. This distribution is slated to occur on or around 2nd June 2025 and will be accessible to all shareholders listed as of 23rd May 2025. Subsequently, DNO shares will be traded ex-dividend starting 22nd May 2025.
news.headline: DNO ASA approves NOK 0.3125/share dividend; payment on 2 June to shareholders as of 23 May, ex-dividend trading from 22 May.

news.summary: In Oxford, England, as of May 15, 2025, an increasing number of American families are contemplating relocating to Europe and the UK for various reasons such as lifestyle enhancements, cultural exposure, and employment adaptability. Education has notably become a crucial factor in their decision-making. Private tutoring services in the UK are witnessing more attention from these families as they prepare for their potential move.
news.headline: Increase in US families moving to Europe leads to rise in educational support requests for UK-based private tutoring services.

news.summary: Recent findings reveal stark disparities in unemployment rates among youth and young adults in Chicago, Cook County, and Illinois. According to a report from the University of, over 80% of teenagers in certain areas of Chicago are currently unemployed. This data underscores the significant challenges faced by individuals in accessing employment opportunities, particularly in these regions. Such inequities call for targeted interventions to address the underlying factors contributing to this issue.
news.headline: Youth unemployment exceeds 80% in parts of Chicago, highlighting racial and geographic inequities.

news.summary: Norwegian oil and gas operator, DNO ASA, announced an operating profit of USD 28 million for the first quarter of 2025, with revenues reaching USD 188 million during the same period. These figures reflect a significant quarter-on-quarter growth for the company.
news.headline: DNO ASA reports Q1 2025 operating profit of USD 28 million on USD 188 million revenues, both with QoQ growth. Prepares to explore Norwegian waters.

news.summary: XUZHOU, China - On May 15, 2025, XCMG Machinery, a prominent company in construction machinery and equipment (SHE:000425), revealed plans for the 7th XCMG International Customer Festival happening from May 20th to 22nd in Xuzhou, China. The event will focus on the theme "Solid...".
news.headline: XCMG plans 7th International Customer Festival in 2025 in Xuzhou, China from May 20-22, focusing on innovation and sustainability.

news.summary: During the CoinDesk's Consensus 2025 conference, Anthony Scaramucci, founder and CEO of SkyBridge Capital, shared his view that Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) will be recognized as a full-fledged asset class when its value reaches $500,000 per coin. He compared the market capitalization milestones of different asset classes, stating that $3 trillion is comparable to a top-tier stock while $20 trillion signifies an asset class. Scaramucci highlighted the significance of Bitcoin reaching a market cap equivalent to gold, which currently stands at over $21 trillion, in order to be considered a distinct asset class. He also mentioned the increasing capital flow into Bitcoin through exchange-traded funds and the adoption of certain strategies.
news.headline: SkyBridge Capital CEO Anthony Scaramucci believes Bitcoin will be considered an asset class at $500,000 a piece, to match gold's market capitalization of $21 trillion.

news.summary: On May 3, 2025, the Ministry of Economic Affairs in Taiwan organized the first Best AI Award Competition at Taipei World Trade Center, Hall 1. The event aimed to enhance Taiwan's AI talent pool and draw more attention to the field of artificial intelligence.
news.headline: Taiwan's MOEA hosts inaugural AI Awards, celebrating best AI minds on May 3, 2025 at Taipei World Trade Center, Hall 1.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, in New York, Yasmine Sherif, the Executive Director of Education Cannot Wait, released a statement in honor of the International Day of Families. She emphasized the significance of family as a unifying and supportive force during challenging times. Sherif highlighted the crucial role families play in connecting generations and cultures. She stressed the unparalleled power of family in providing strength and cohesion. The statement underscores the importance of recognizing and celebrating the importance of families worldwide.
news.headline: International Day of Families statement by Education Cannot Wait Director emphasizes family's role in fostering peace & development.

news.summary: e.l.f. Beauty is continuing to grow its presence in The Netherlands and Belgium by expanding e.l.f. Cosmetics and e.l.f. SKIN products in KRUIDVAT and TREKPLEISTER stores. This move is part of e.l.f.'s mission to provide quality beauty products to a wider audience. The brand is currently operating in 15 countries and is globally distributed through eShopWorld. The company's success outside of the U.S. market is evident, and there is still considerable room for growth. The expansion in Q3 signifies the company's commitment to reaching more customers.
news.headline: e.l.f. Beauty expands into drugstores in Netherlands and Belgium, reaching 15 countries with global distribution.

news.summary: Corona has once again been named the top beer brand globally for the second year in a row in Kantar's BrandZ 2025 Most Valuable Global Brands report. Out of the top 10 most valuable beer brands worldwide, eight are owned by AB InBev. The company, listed as Brussel:ABI, BMV:ANB, JSE:ANH, NYSE:BUD, reported record-high revenue and a 15% increase in Underlying EPS in 2024. This year saw a double-digit growth trend for AB InBev, solidifying its position as a leading player in the global beer market.
news.headline: Corona Global tops Kantar BrandZ rankings as most valuable beer brand for 2nd year. AB InBev owns 8/10 top brands.2024 saw record revenue and 15% EPS growth.

news.summary: VEON Ltd. reported strong financial performance for the first quarter of 2025, with total revenue increasing by 8.9% year-on-year to USD 1,026 million, and EBITDA growing by 13.7% year-on-year to USD 439 million. Direct digital revenue surged by 50.2% year-on-year to USD 147 million, comprising 14.3% of total revenues. The company's total cash equivalents amounted to USD 1,775 million, with net debt decreasing to USD 1,810 million. VEON is maintaining its outlook for 2025 with expected revenue growth between 12% and 14% and EBITDA growth between 13% and 15%. The company also announced a new share buyback program of up to USD 35 million in addition to the completed USD 30 million first phase. CEO Kaan Terzioglu emphasized the company's focus on innovation and strategic clarity in delivering digital services.
news.headline: VEON reports strong 1Q25 earnings with 50% surge in digital revenues to USD 147 million, total revenues at USD 1,026 million.

news.summary: In recent developments, a major technological company announced its plans to invest $1 billion into a groundbreaking project aimed at developing innovative solutions for renewable energy. The company stated that this investment will create over 1,000 new jobs in the clean energy sector and contribute to reducing carbon emissions by 20% in the next five years. The project is set to launch next month and is expected to revolutionize the way renewable energy is produced and utilized. This initiative highlights the company's commitment to sustainability and addressing climate change challenges.
news.headline: Investigation underway for Medicare fraud at UnitedHealth by authorities; significant numbers targeted.
